title: Molten Middle Eastern Red Peppers Muhammara
description: A citrusy and molten Middle Eastern dish featuring red peppers and Appleton rum, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This gluten-free friendly recipe is made in a pressure cooker and served in a cazuela.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the chopped garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red bell peppers and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the Appleton rum, lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Close the lid and cook for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally, then open the lid and stir in the chopped walnuts.
Transfer the muhammara to a cazuela and serve hot.
Garnish with additional walnuts and red pepper flakes, if desired.
Serve with pita bread or crackers for a delicious and easy dinner.
